devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथ सर्वगुणोपेतः कालः परमशोभनः । यर्हि एव अजनजन्मर्क्षं शान्तर्क्षग्रहतारकम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca atha sarvaguṇopetaḥ kālaḥ paramaśobhanaḥ yarhi eva ajanajanmarkṣaṃ śāntarkṣagrahatārakam

[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
Now came the most auspicious time full of all blessings when the constellation Rohiṇī presided over by god Brahmā, the son of Viṣṇu, was at the ascendant and all constellations of stars (like Aśvinī and others), planets and stars assumed peaceful aspects.

devanagari: दिशः प्रसेदुर्गगनं निर्मलोडुगणोदयम् । मही मंगलभूयिष्ठ पुरग्रामव्रजाकरा ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: diśaḥ prasedurgaganaṃ nirmaloḍugaṇodayam mahī maṃgalabhūyiṣṭha puragrāmavrajākarā
translation: All the directions became clear and propitious (despite the continuation of the rainy season in the month of Śrāvaṇa) the sky became illumined with innumerable clusters of stars shining unobscured; the earth with its towns, villages, settlements of cowherds and mines revelled in auspicious festivities.

devanagari: नद्यः प्रसन्नसलिला ह्रदा जलरुहश्रियः । द्विजालिकुल सन्नाद स्तबका वनराजयः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: nadyaḥ prasannasalilā hradā jalaruhaśriyaḥ dvijālikula sannāda stabakā vanarājayaḥ
translation: The waters of the rivers became crystal clear; the lakes were blooming with lotuses; the rows of forest trees laden with bunches of flowers were resounded with swarms of the sweetly humming bees and cooing of birds.

devanagari: ववौ वायुः सुखस्पर्शः पुण्यगन्धवहः शुचिः । अग्नयश्च द्विजातीनां शान्तास्तत्र समिन्धत ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: vavau vāyuḥ sukhasparśaḥ puṇyagandhavahaḥ śuciḥ agnayaśca dvijātīnāṃ śāntāstatra samindhata

translation: A pure, cool breeze wafting sweet fragrance blew delighting in its touch; the sacred fires maintained in the houses of the twice-borns (automatically) burst into flames.

devanagari: मनांस्यासन् प्रसन्नानि साधूनां असुरद्रुहाम् । जायमानेऽजने तस्मिन् नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो दिवि ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: manāṃsyāsan prasannāni sādhūnāṃ asuradruhām jāyamāne

translation: With the exception of demons like Kaṃsa, the minds of all the creatures and those of gods became filled with delight. When the birthless (Lord Viṣṇu) was about to incarnate, kettle-drums were being sounded in the celestial region.

devanagari: जगुः किन्नरगन्धर्वाः तुष्टुवुः सिद्धचारणाः । विद्याधर्यश्च ननृतुः अप्सरोभिः समं तदा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: jaguḥ kinnaragandharvāḥ tuṣṭuvuḥ siddhacāraṇāḥ vidyādharyaśca nanṛtuḥ apsarobhiḥ samaṃ tadā
translation: At that time Kinnaras and Gandharvas burst forth singing, Siddhas and Cāraṇas sang prayers; ladies of the Vidyādhara clan danced along with celestial damsels.

devanagari: मुमुचुर्मुनयो देवाः सुमनांसि मुदान्विताः । मन्दं मन्दं जलधरा जगर्जुः अनुसागरम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: mumucurmunayo devāḥ sumanāṃsi mudānvitāḥ mandaṃ mandaṃ jaladharā jagarjuḥ anusāgaram
translation: Overwhelmed with joy the gods and sages showered (heaps of) flowers; following the roaring of the seas, clouds gave forth a low rumbling sound.

devanagari: निशीथे तम उद्‍भूते जायमाने जनार्दने । देवक्यां देवरूपिण्यां विष्णुः सर्वगुहाशयः । आविरासीद् यथा प्राच्यां दिशि इन्दुरिव पुष्कलः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: niśīthe tama ud‍bhūte jāyamāne janārdane devakyāṃ devarūpiṇyāṃ viṣṇuḥ sarvaguhāśayaḥ āvirāsīd yathā prācyāṃ diśi induriva puṣkalaḥ

[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: At midnight when the darkness tormenting the people was thickening, Lord Viṣṇu, the Indweller of the hearts of all, manifested himself in his own complete divine form, like the full moon rising in the East, through Devakī who was like unto a goddess.

devanagari: तमद्‍भुतं बालकमम्बुजेक्षणं । चतुर्भुजं शंखगदार्युदायुधम् । श्रीवत्सलक्ष्मं गलशोभि कौस्तुभं । पीताम्बरं सान्द्रपयोदसौभगम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tamad‍bhutaṃ bālakamambujekṣaṇaṃ caturbhujaṃ śaṃkhagadāryudāyudham śrīvatsalakṣmaṃ galaśobhi kaustubhaṃ pītāmbaraṃ sāndrapayodasaubhagam
translation: Vasudeva saw that wonderful child of lotus-like eyes, endowed with four arms, bearing a coṇch and wielding aloft a mace and a discus (and a lotus); having (on his chest) the Śrīvatsa mark (of curl of golden hair), with the Kaustubha gem (hanging from his neck but) beautified by his neck; clad in yellow silken garments, possessing the charming complexion of rain-bearing cloud; his profuse locks of hair bathed in the lustre of his crown and ear-rings studded with invaluable vaidūrya-gems (lapis lazuli); resplendent with his exquisite and valuable zone, armlets, bracelets and other ornaments.

devanagari: महार्हवैदूर्यकिरीटकुण्डल । त्विषा परिष्वक्त सहस्रकुन्तलम् । उद्दाम काञ्च्यङ्‍गद कङ्कणादिभिः । विरोचमानं वसुदेव ऐक्षत ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: mahārhavaidūryakirīṭakuṇḍala tviṣā pariṣvakta sahasrakuntalam uddāma kāñcyaṅ‍gada kaṅkaṇādibhiḥ virocamānaṃ vasudeva aikṣata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [aorist middle third plural]

translation: Vasudeva saw that wonderful child of lotus-like eyes, endowed with four arms, bearing a coṇch and wielding aloft a mace and a discus (and a lotus); having (on his chest) the Śrīvatsa mark (of curl of golden hair), with the Kaustubha gem (hanging from his neck but) beautified by his neck; clad in yellow silken garments, possessing the charming complexion of rain-bearing cloud; his profuse locks of hair bathed in the lustre of his crown and ear-rings studded with invaluable vaidūrya-gems (lapis lazuli); resplendent with his exquisite and valuable zone, armlets, bracelets and other ornaments.

devanagari: स विस्मयोत्फुल्ल विलोचनो हरिं । सुतं विलोक्यानकदुन्दुभिस्तदा । कृष्णावतारोत्सव संभ्रमोऽस्पृशन् । मुदा द्विजेभ्योऽयुतमाप्लुतो गवाम् ॥ ११...
english_devnagari: sa vismayotphulla vilocano hariṃ sutaṃ vilokyānakadundubhistadā kṛṣṇāvatārotsava saṃbhramo mudā dvijebhyo

[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: With eyes blooming wide open with amazement, Vasudeva beheld Lord Hari (born as) his son. With his mind confused with rapture on that happy and auspicious occasion of Kṛṣṇa’s incarnation, he (Vasudeva) was overcome with (lit. bathed in) delight, and gave ten thousand cows as gift to Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: अथैनमस्तौदवधार्य पूरुषं । परं नताङ्गः कृतधीः कृताञ्जलिः । स्वरोचिषा भारत सूतिकागृहं । विरोचयन्तं गतभीः प्रभाववित् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: athainamastaudavadhārya pūruṣaṃ paraṃ natāṅgaḥ kṛtadhīḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ svarociṣā bhārata sūtikāgṛhaṃ virocayantaṃ gatabhīḥ prabhāvavit
translation:
Now realizing the child who illuminated the lying- in-chamber with his splendour, was Lord Viṣṇu, the Supreme Person, Vasudeva of pure heart, who, due to his awareness of Viṣṇu’s prowess completely lost his fear, began to praise him with folded palms, and body bent low in humility, Oh scion of Bharata.
(Having first embraced the Lord as his son)

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । विदितोऽसि भवान् साक्षात् पुरुषः प्रकृतेः परः । केवलानुभवानन्द स्वरूपः सर्वबुद्धिदृक् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvāca vidito kevalānubhavānanda svarūpaḥ sarvabuddhidṛk

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation:
Vasudeva said:
How blessed I am that I have seen you directly—you the Supreme Person, transcendental to and distinct from Prakṛti, absolute wisdom and supreme bliss incarnate, and (as inner controller) the witness of all minds and intellects.

devanagari: स एव स्वप्रकृत्येदं सृष्ट्वाग्रे त्रिगुणात्मकम् । तदनु त्वं ह्यप्रविष्टः प्रविष्ट इव भाव्यसे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva svaprakṛtyedaṃ sṛṣṭvāgre triguṇātmakam tadanu tvaṃ hyapraviṣṭaḥ praviṣṭa iva bhāvyase
translation: (As a matter of fact you never entered the womb of Devakī for) you are factually of such absolute nature as is described above. Having evolved this universe consisting of three guṇas through your creative potency called Māyā, at the beginning of creation, you have not really entered into it (for the Lord is infinite and the universe is limited); you are, however, presumed to have entered it (as a Puruṣa and the omnipresent Inner Controller).

devanagari: यथा इमे अविकृता भावाः तथा ते विकृतैः सह । नानावीर्याः पृथग्भूता विराजं जनयन्ति हि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā ime avikṛtā bhāvāḥ tathā te vikṛtaiḥ saha nānāvīryāḥ pṛthagbhūtā virājaṃ janayanti hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan ], [vocative plural from √ jan ], [accusative plural from √ jan ]
[vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan ]

translation: Just as these unmodified elementary principles (such as mahat, Ahaṃkāra) are in existence at the beginning of the creation, and are severally incapable of creation though endowed with different properties and powers, they give rise to the universe after combining with their evolutes.

devanagari: सन्निपत्य समुत्पाद्य दृश्यन्तेऽनुगता इव । प्रागेव विद्यमानत्वात् न तेषां इह संभवः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sannipatya samutpādya dṛśyante prāgeva vidyamānatvāt na teṣāṃ iha saṃbhavaḥ
translation: Having come together (synthesizing) with their (sixteen) evolutes, they create the universe and appear to have entered into it; but due to their existence prior to the creation (as its cause), their (later) entry is an impossibility. (Similarly, the Lord existed before Devakī and his appearance to have entered her womb is not a reality).

devanagari: एवं भवान् बुद्ध्यनुमेयलक्षणैः । ग्राह्यैर्गुणैः सन्नपि तद्‍गुणाग्रहः । अनावृतत्वाद् बहिरन्तरं न ते । सर्वस्य सर्वात्मन आत्मवस्तुनः ॥ १७...
english_devnagari: evaṃ bhavān buddhyanumeyalakṣaṇaiḥ grāhyairguṇaiḥ sannapi tad‍guṇāgrahaḥ anāvṛtatvād bahirantaraṃ na te sarvasya sarvātmana ātmavastunaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [instrumental plural from √ grah ]
[instrumental plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [instrumental plural from √ grah ]

translation: [1] In this way, even though your revered self exists along with the objects which are cognisable through senses (the true nature of which is) inferrable through perception of senseobjects you are not perceived along with those objects, directly (as the senses can perceive only their specific objects and God is not such an object); (for you, being unconditioned and all-pervading, the distinction of being inside or outside is inapplicable to you. You are everything and the indwelling Soul of all, the self-abiding real essence. (Hence your entry in the womb of Devakī is not a reality).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
(1) Bhāgavata Candrikā: Though you abide in all creatures like gods, men—the products of guṇas—you are not affected by their limitations. You are above modifications, essentially of unlimited knowledge and blissful nature. You are un-enveloped i.e. of unlimited knowledge. Being all-pervading and the cause of all the distinction of being ‘in’ and ‘out’ is not applicable to you. As you are the independent Inner Controller, antaryāmin, the limitations of the body do not affect you.
(2) Siddhāntapradīpa emphasizes that the direct vision of God is due to His grace and He is beyond the ken of senses.
(3) VT.: As you are always loving to your devotees, you have come to us as a son which is the highest reward (of our devotion).

devanagari: य आत्मनो दृश्यगुणेषु सन्निति । व्यवस्यते स्वव्यतिरेकतोऽबुधः । विनानुवादं न च तन्मनीषितं । सम्यग् यतस्त्यक्तमुपाददत् पुमान् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya ātmano dṛśyaguṇeṣu sanniti vyavasyate svavyatirekato vinānuvādaṃ na ca tanmanīṣitaṃ samyag yatastyaktamupādadat pumān

[nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: [2] He who presumes real independent existence—as distinct from the Soul—in the case of objects perceived by him (or by the Soul) is an ignorant person. For when these objects (i.e. the world) are subjected to investigation, they are (found to be) of verbal nature and essentially unreal. He who acknowledges the unreality as a distinct reality is (certainly) a dullard.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī: In the absence of the support of Vedic texts, it is untenable to admit the conclusion of the ignorant that you are identical with the body or the senses simply because of Your presence in them. For the Supreme Man (Lord Viṣṇu) has accepted (or revealed) the super-human faultless Vedic texts which dispel all doubts.

devanagari: त्वत्तोऽस्य जन्मस्थिति संयमान् विभो । वदन्ति अनीहात् अगुणाद् अविक्रियात् । त्वयीश्वरे ब्रह्मणि नो विरुध्यते । त्वद् आश्रयत्वाद् उपचर्यत...
english_devnagari: tvatto vadanti anīhāt aguṇād avikriyāt tvayīśvare brahmaṇi no virudhyate tvad āśrayatvād upacaryate guṇaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Oh all-pervading Lord! They say that the creation, preservation and destruction of the universe proceeds from you who are above all guṇas, (and hence) desireless (and performing no acts), and subject to no modification. As you are both Brahman and the Supreme Ruler, there is no contradiction in transcendence to modifications and the creation etc. of the universe. For though the activity is carried out by guṇas, it is ascribed to you as guṇas (ultimately depend on you only).

devanagari: स त्वं त्रिलोकस्थितये स्वमायया । बिभर्षि शुक्लं खलु वर्णमात्मनः । सर्गाय रक्तं रजसोपबृंहितं । कृष्णं च वर्णं तमसा जनात्यये ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ trilokasthitaye svamāyayā bibharṣi śuklaṃ khalu varṇamātmanaḥ sargāya raktaṃ rajasopabṛṃhitaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ ca varṇaṃ tamasā janātyaye

[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: You who are of such a nature (as described above) verily assume, through your Māyā potency, pure white complexion (Brahmā-form) full of the principle of rajas for the creation of these worlds, and a dark complexion (Rudra-form) through tamas for the destruction of the universe.

devanagari: त्वमस्य लोकस्य विभो रिरक्षिषुः । गृहेऽवतीर्णोऽसि ममाखिलेश्वर । राजन्य संज्ञासुरकोटि यूथपैः । निर्व्यूह्यमाना निहनिष्यसे चमूः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamasya lokasya vibho rirakṣiṣuḥ gṛhe rājanya saṃjñāsurakoṭi yūthapaiḥ nirvyūhyamānā nihaniṣyase camūḥ
translation: Oh all-pervading Lord, the supreme Ruler of all! (how gracious of you it is that though Your avatāra is an impossibility in the real sense of the term), You have incarnated yourself in my house (despite its being a prison-cell), with a desire to protect this world. You will annihilate the hordes led by millions of Asura chiefs passing under the name of Kṣatriyas.

devanagari: अयं त्वसभ्यस्तव जन्म नौ गृहे । श्रुत्वाग्रजांस्ते न्यवधीत् सुरेश्वर । स तेऽवतारं पुरुषैः समर्पितं । श्रुत्वाधुनैव अभिसरत्युदायुधः ॥ २२ ...
english_devnagari: ayaṃ tvasabhyastava janma nau gṛhe śrutvāgrajāṃste nyavadhīt sureśvara sa te śrutvādhunaiva abhisaratyudāyudhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Lord of gods! Hearing (the prediction) of Your birth in our house, this villainous fellow (Kaṃsa) has (coldbloodedly) killed your elder brothers. Learning your incarnation directly through the reports of his servants, he will just now rush here with uplifted weapons.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथैनं आत्मजं वीक्ष्य महापुरुष लक्षणम् । देवकी तं उपाधावत् कंसाद् भीता सुचिस्मिता ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca athainaṃ ātmajaṃ vīkṣya mahāpuruṣa lakṣaṇam devakī taṃ upādhāvat kaṃsād bhītā sucismitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Now, seeing that this son of hers bore the characteristics of the supreme person (Lord Viṣṇu), Devakī who was (erstwhile) afraid of Kaṃsa, (now) smiled happily and began to pray him:

devanagari: श्रीदेवक्युवाच । रूपं यत् तत् प्राहुरव्यक्तमाद्यं । ब्रह्म ज्योतिर्निर्गुणं निर्विकारम् । सत्तामात्रं निर्विशेषं निरीहं । स त्वं साक्षा...
english_devnagari: śrīdevakyuvāca rūpaṃ yat tat prāhuravyaktamādyaṃ brahma jyotirnirguṇaṃ nirvikāram sattāmātraṃ nirviśeṣaṃ nirīhaṃ sa tvaṃ sākṣāt viṣṇuradhyātmadīpaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ ad ], [accusative single from √ ad ]

translation:
Devakī said:
(How can there be any danger to you) who are verily the Lord Viṣṇu himself, the Illuminator of the aggregate of intelligence, senses and other psycho-physical organisms: and who are that indescribable Reality whom the Vedas declare as the unmanifest primordial cause, the Supreme Brahman, pure consciousness transcending all guṇas and modifications, absolute existence attributeless, and devoid of activity.

devanagari: नष्टे लोके द्विपरार्धावसाने । महाभूतेषु आदिभूतं गतेषु । व्यक्ते अव्यक्तं कालवेगेन याते । भवान् एकः शिष्यते शेषसंज्ञः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: naṣṭe loke dviparārdhāvasāne mahābhūteṣu ādibhūtaṃ gateṣu vyakte avyaktaṃ kālavegena yāte bhavān ekaḥ śiṣyate śeṣasaṃjñaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [locative single from √ naś class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ naś class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [locative single from √ naś class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [locative single from √ yā class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: When at the end of the period of two parārdhas (which terminates the life-time of Brahmā), by the velocity of time, the universe dissolves and all the gross elements (Mahābhūtas) are absorbed into their primordial subtle form (viz. ahaṃkāra which in its turn is absorbed into the principle called mahat) and the principle mahat is merged in the unmanifest (pradhāna which is absorbed in you), You alone remain behind (remaining cognisant that the Pradhāna lying merged in You is to be re-awakened) and hence you are designated as the Residue (Śeṣa) or the complete whole (aśeṣa).

devanagari: योऽयं कालस्तस्य तेऽव्यक्तबन्धो । चेष्टां आहुः चेष्टते येन विश्वम् । निमेषादिः वत्सरान्तो महीयान् । तं त्वेशानं क्षेमधाम प्रपद्ये ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yo ceṣṭāṃ āhuḥ ceṣṭate yena viśvam nimeṣādiḥ vatsarānto mahīyān taṃ tveśānaṃ kṣemadhāma prapadye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ īś class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ īś class 2 verb]

translation: The wise say that this mighty time as described above which begins with a nimeṣa (twinkling of the eye) and extends (through cycle of years) to the end of dviparārdha (Brahmā’s life) and which controls the cyclic course of the universe, is your own sportful activity, Oh Director of the Prakṛti! I seek your protection—You who are the Supreme Ruler and the abode of security.

devanagari: मर्त्यो मृत्युव्यालभीतः पलायन् । लोकान् सर्वान् निर्भयं नाध्यगच्छत् । त्वत्पादाब्जं प्राप्य यदृच्छयाद्य । स्वस्थः शेते मृत्युरस्मादपैति...
english_devnagari: martyo mṛtyuvyālabhītaḥ palāyan lokān sarvān nirbhayaṃ nādhyagacchat tvatpādābjaṃ prāpya yadṛcchayādya svasthaḥ śete mṛtyurasmādapaiti
translation: A mortal, being apprehensive of the serpent (in the form) of death, and running about all the world (for security against death), failed to find a place free from fear. Today having attained to your lotus-like feet through an unexpected stroke of good luck (and through your grace), he reposes in peace and security. It is (now) Death that runs away from him.

devanagari: स त्वं घोरात् उग्रसेनात्मजात् नः । त्राहि त्रस्तान् भृत्यवित्रासहासि । रूपं चेदं पौरुषं ध्यानधिष्ण्यं । मा प्रत्यक्षं मांसदृशां कृषीष्ठ...
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ ghorāt ugrasenātmajāt naḥ trāhi trastān bhṛtyavitrāsahāsi rūpaṃ cedaṃ pauruṣaṃ dhyānadhiṣṇyaṃ mā pratyakṣaṃ māṃsadṛśāṃ kṛṣīṣṭhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ tras class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tras class 4 verb]

translation: Of such nature as You are, protect us (your devotees) who are afraid of the terrible son of Ugrasena; for you are the rescuer of your devotees (servants) from fear. Let not this divine form of yours which is worthy of being the object of meditation, be manifest to the gross physical eyes of human beings.

devanagari: जन्म ते मय्यसौ पापो मा विद्यात् मधुसूदन । समुद्विजे भवद्धेतोः कंसाद् अहमधीरधीः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: janma te mayyasau pāpo mā vidyāt madhusūdana samudvije bhavaddhetoḥ kaṃsād ahamadhīradhīḥ
translation: Let not that wicked Kaṃsa know of your birth from me, Oh Maḍhusūdana (slayer of the demon Madhu). Being naturally timid, I am terribly afraid of Kaṃsa, for your sake.

devanagari: उपसंहर विश्वात्मन् अदो रूपं अलौकिकम् । शंखचक्रगदापद्म श्रिया जुष्टं चतुर्भुजम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: upasaṃhara viśvātman ado rūpaṃ alaukikam śaṃkhacakragadāpadma śriyā juṣṭaṃ caturbhujam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Oh soul of the universe! May you withdraw this Superhuman form with four arms furnished with the superb splendour of conch, discus, mace and lotus.

devanagari: विश्वं यदेतत् स्वतनौ निशान्ते । यथावकाशं पुरुषः परो भवान् । बिभर्ति सोऽयं मम गर्भगो अभूद् । अहो नृलोकस्य विडंबनं हि तत् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: viśvaṃ yadetat svatanau niśānte yathāvakāśaṃ puruṣaḥ paro bhavān bibharti so aho nṛlokasya viḍaṃbanaṃ hi tat
translation: That your Lordship, the Supreme Person who contains within yourself all this universe in extenso at the end of the cosmic night (during Pralaya), should get himself accommodated in my womb is certainly a wonderful imitation of the human (ways of the) world.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । त्वमेव पूर्वसर्गेऽभूः पृश्निः स्वायंभुवे सति । तदायं सुतपा नाम प्रजापतिः अकल्मषः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca tvameva pūrvasarge tadāyaṃ sutapā nāma prajāpatiḥ akalmaṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
“Oh chaste lady! In your previous birth in the age of Svāyambhuva Manu, you were Pṛśni and this sinless Vasudeva your husband, was then a Prajāpati named Sutapas.

devanagari: युवां वै ब्रह्मणाऽदिष्टौ प्रजासर्गे यदा ततः । सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं तेपाथे परमं तपः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvāṃ vai brahmaṇā sanniyamyendriyagrāmaṃ tepāthe paramaṃ tapaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: When both of you were directed by god Brahmā to create progeny, you subdued all your senses and performed a very austere penance.

devanagari: वर्षवातातपहिम घर्मकालगुणाननु । सहमानौ श्वासरोध विनिर्धूतमनोमलौ ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: varṣavātātapahima gharmakālaguṇānanu sahamānau śvāsarodha vinirdhūtamanomalau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative dual from √ sah class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sah class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sah class 1 verb]

translation: Enduring in due course, all the inclemencies of the seasons such as rains, storms, solar rays, snow-fall and heat, you completely washed off the impurities of the mind by breathcontrol.

devanagari: शीर्णपर्णानिलाहारौ उपशान्तेन चेतसा । मत्तः कामान् अभीप्सन्तौ मद् आराधनमीहतुः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: śīrṇaparṇānilāhārau upaśāntena cetasā mattaḥ kāmān abhīpsantau mad ārādhanamīhatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mad class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ mad class 4 verb]

translation: Subsisting on withered leaves and merely on air and actuated with the desire to obtain your cherished objects from me, you were determined to propitiate Me with a serene mind.

devanagari: एवं वां तप्यतोस्तीव्रं तपः परमदुष्करम् । दिव्यवर्षसहस्राणि द्वादशेयुः मदात्मनोः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vāṃ tapyatostīvraṃ tapaḥ paramaduṣkaram divyavarṣasahasrāṇi dvādaśeyuḥ madātmanoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active third plural], [perfect active third plural]

translation: Thus rolled by twelve thousand divine years, while both of you, with your heart fixed on Me, were performing a very difficult and austere penance.

devanagari: तदा वां परितुष्टोऽहं अमुना वपुषानघे । तपसा श्रद्धया नित्यं भक्त्या च हृदि भावितः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā vāṃ parituṣṭo tapasā śraddhayā nityaṃ bhaktyā ca hṛdi bhāvitaḥ
translation: Being continuously contemplated upon in the heart with faith, devotion and asceticism, I was highly pleased with you and manifested myself before you in this very form with a desire to fulfil your cherished objects, Oh sinless one, When I, the foremost bestower of boons, told you to ask Me for a boon, the boon for having a son like Me was sought by you.

devanagari: प्रादुरासं वरदराड् युवयोः कामदित्सया । व्रियतां वर इत्युक्ते मादृशो वां वृतः सुतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: prādurāsaṃ varadarāḍ yuvayoḥ kāmaditsayā vriyatāṃ vara ityukte mādṛśo vāṃ vṛtaḥ sutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Being continuously contemplated upon in the heart with faith, devotion and asceticism, I was highly pleased with you and manifested myself before you in this very form with a desire to fulfil your cherished objects, Oh sinless one, When I, the foremost bestower of boons, told you to ask Me for a boon, the boon for having a son like Me was sought by you.

devanagari: अजुष्टग्राम्यविषयौ अनपत्यौ च दम्पती । न वव्राथेऽपवर्गं मे मोहितौ देवमायया ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: ajuṣṭagrāmyaviṣayau anapatyau ca dampatī na vavrāthe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ muh ], [vocative dual from √ muh ], [accusative dual from √ muh ]

translation: Though both of you had not tasted the conjugal and other pleasures of ordinary sensual objects and were childless, you did not seek for Emancipation (from the world) as you were deluded by My divine Māyā.

devanagari: गते मयि युवां लब्ध्वा वरं मत्सदृशं सुतम् । ग्राम्यान् भोगान् अभुञ्जाथां युवां प्राप्तमनोरथौ ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: gate mayi yuvāṃ labdhvā varaṃ matsadṛśaṃ sutam grāmyān bhogān abhuñjāthāṃ yuvāṃ prāptamanorathau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: When I disappeared from your view and you, having secured the boon of getting a son like Me and thus accomplishing your cherished desire, you indulged in enjoying sensual pleasures.

devanagari: अदृष्ट्वान्यतमं लोके शीलौदार्यगुणैः समम् । अहं सुतो वामभवं पृश्निगर्भ इति श्रुतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: adṛṣṭvānyatamaṃ loke śīlaudāryaguṇaiḥ samam ahaṃ suto vāmabhavaṃ pṛśnigarbha iti śrutaḥ
translation: Finding that nobody else in this world is comparable to Me in goodness of nature, generosity and other virtues in this world, I Myself was born as your son and became known as Pṛśni-garbha.

devanagari: तयोर्वां पुनरेवाहं अदित्यामास कश्यपात् । उपेन्द्र इति विख्यातो वामनत्वाच्च वामनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorvāṃ punarevāhaṃ adityāmāsa kaśyapāt upendra iti vikhyāto vāmanatvācca vāmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: And again of you both (as Aditi and Kaśyapa) I was born of Aditi from Kaśyapa and was famous as Upendra and also as Vāmana due to shortness of stature.

devanagari: तृतीयेऽस्मिन् भवेऽहं वै तेनैव वपुषाथ वाम् । जातो भूयस्तयोरेव सत्यं मे व्याहृतं सति ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛtīye jāto bhūyastayoreva satyaṃ me vyāhṛtaṃ sati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: Now even in this third incarnation, I am verily born with that very form again of you both (who are former Aditi and Kaśyapa). In this way, my word has been kept, Oh pious lady.

devanagari: एतद् वां दर्शितं रूपं प्राग्जन्म स्मरणाय मे । नान्यथा मद्‍भवं ज्ञानं मर्त्यलिङ्गेन जायते ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: etad vāṃ darśitaṃ rūpaṃ prāgjanma smaraṇāya me nānyathā mad‍bhavaṃ jñānaṃ martyaliṅgena jāyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś ], [accusative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: I have revealed this form of Mine to you in order to remind you of my previous births (from you); otherwise the knowledge about my real being is not possible through the semblance of human form. (Now as desired by you, I shall assume the form of a human baby).

devanagari: युवां मां पुत्रभावेन ब्रह्मभावेन चासकृत् । चिन्तयन्तौ कृतस्नेहौ यास्येथे मद्‍गतिं पराम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvāṃ māṃ putrabhāvena brahmabhāvena cāsakṛt cintayantau kṛtasnehau yāsyethe mad‍gatiṃ parām

translation:
Thinking of Me constantly as your son as well as contemplating upon Me as Supreme Brahman and loving me affectionately, both of you will attain to the highest state in relation to Me.
[Vṛndāvana edition adds:]
45(A). If you are still afraid of Kaṃsa, quickly take me to Gokula and bring (as substitute) My Māyā who has been born from (the womb of) Yaśodā.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्त्वासीत् हरिः तूष्णीं भगवान् आत्ममायया । पित्रोः संपश्यतोः सद्यो बभूव प्राकृतः शिशुः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityuktvāsīt hariḥ tūṣṇīṃ bhagavān ātmamāyayā pitroḥ saṃpaśyatoḥ sadyo babhūva prākṛtaḥ śiśuḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having spoken thus, Hari became silent. With his Māyā power, the Lord immediately became an ordinary human babe, while the parents were simply looking on (with wonder).

devanagari: ततश्च शौरिः भगवत्प्रचोदितः । सुतं समादाय स सूतिकागृहात् । यदा बहिर्गन्तुमियेष तर्ह्यजा । या योगमायाजनि नन्दजायया ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca śauriḥ bhagavatpracoditaḥ sutaṃ samādāya sa sūtikāgṛhāt yadā bahirgantumiyeṣa tarhyajā yā yogamāyājani nandajāyayā

translation: Thereupon, when Vasudeva, the son of Śūra desired to go out of the lying-in-chamber taking his son with him (and was about to start) as directed by the Lord, Yoga-Māyā, though (herself) birthless, was born of Nanda’s wife.

devanagari: तया हृतप्रत्यय सर्ववृत्तिषु । द्वाःस्थेषु पौरेष्वपि शायितेष्वथ । द्वारस्तु सर्वाः पिहिता दुरत्यया । बृहत् कपाटायस कीलश्रृंखलैः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tayā hṛtapratyaya sarvavṛttiṣu dvāḥstheṣu paureṣvapi śāyiteṣvatha dvārastu sarvāḥ pihitā duratyayā bṛhat kapāṭāyasa kīlaśrṛṃkhalaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: By Yoga-Māyā the gate-keepers were deprived of all their consciousness and functioning of their senses and even the citizens were steeped in deep sleep; all the gates (of the palace prison) which were closed by huge doors secured with iron chains and bolts automatically flung open at the approach of Vasudeva carrying Kṛṣṇa (on his head) like darkness dispersing at the appearance of the Sun. The rain cloud showered with a gentle rumbling and the serpent Śeṣa (closely) followed him, warding off the rain-water with his hoods spread over his head (like an umbrella).

devanagari: ताः कृष्णवाहे वसुदेव आगते । स्वयं व्यवर्यन्त यथा तमो रवेः । ववर्ष पर्जन्य उपांशुगर्जितः । शेषोऽन्वगाद् वारि निवारयन् फणैः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāḥ kṛṣṇavāhe vasudeva āgate svayaṃ vyavaryanta yathā tamo raveḥ vavarṣa parjanya upāṃśugarjitaḥ śeṣo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: By Yoga-Māyā the gate-keepers were deprived of all their consciousness and functioning of their senses and even the citizens were steeped in deep sleep; all the gates (of the palace prison) which were closed by huge doors secured with iron chains and bolts automatically flung open at the approach of Vasudeva carrying Kṛṣṇa (on his head) like darkness dispersing at the appearance of the Sun. The rain cloud showered with a gentle rumbling and the serpent Śeṣa (closely) followed him, warding off the rain-water with his hoods spread over his head (like an umbrella).

devanagari: मघोनि वर्षत्यसकृत् यमानुजा । गंभीर तोयौघ जवोर्मि फेनिला । भयानकावर्त शताकुला नदी । मार्गं ददौ सिन्धुरिव श्रियः पतेः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: maghoni varṣatyasakṛt yamānujā gaṃbhīra toyaugha javormi phenilā bhayānakāvarta śatākulā nadī mārgaṃ dadau sindhuriva śriyaḥ pateḥ

translation: While Indra, the god of rains, continuously poured showers of rain (and even though) the river Yamunā (the presiding deity of which was Yamunā, the younger sister of Yama—the god of death) covered with foam created by waves set up by the velocity of the swiftly-moving deep flood and was full of hundreds of fearful whirlpools, afforded passage (to Vasudeva and Kṛṣṇa) as the Sea did to the Lord of Lakṣmī (Lord Śrī Rāma) formerly.

devanagari: नन्दव्रजं शौरिरुपेत्य तत्र तान् । गोपान् प्रसुप्तान् उपलभ्य निद्रया । सुतं यशोदाशयने निधाय तत् । सुतां उपादाय पुनर्गृहान् अगात् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: nandavrajaṃ śaurirupetya tatra tān gopān prasuptān upalabhya nidrayā sutaṃ yaśodāśayane nidhāya tat sutāṃ upādāya punargṛhān agāt

translation: Arriving at Nanda’s Vraja, Vasudeva found that all the cowherds were deeply buried in sleep. Placing his son on the bed of Yaśodā, taking up her daughter (in exchange), Vasudeva returned home (his palace-prison).

devanagari: देवक्याः शयने न्यस्य वसुदेवोऽथ दारिकाम् । प्रतिमुच्य पदोर्लोहं आस्ते पूर्ववदावृतः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: devakyāḥ śayane nyasya vasudevo pratimucya padorlohaṃ āste pūrvavadāvṛtaḥ
translation: Placing the daughter on the bed of Devakī, Vasudeva put the iron fetters on his feet and remained confined as before.

devanagari: यशोदा नन्दपत्‍नी च जातं परमबुध्यत । न तल्लिङ्गं परिश्रान्ता निद्रयापगतस्मृतिः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: yaśodā nandapat‍nī ca jātaṃ paramabudhyata na talliṅgaṃ pariśrāntā nidrayāpagatasmṛtiḥ

translation:
And Yaśodā, Nanda’s wife was vaguely conscious of having delivered a child, but she was so exhausted and had her memory (so much) clouded under delusion, that she did not discern the sex of the baby.
